title: DLRS .life Package Descriptor
description: Top descriptor (life-package.json) of a .life archive — the portable distribution unit defined by the .life Archive + Runtime Standard (epic #79). Authoritative spec is docs/LIFE_FILE_STANDARD.md. A .life is a zip archive whose top-level life-package.json MUST validate against this schema. Two modes: pointer (default, privacy-preserving — raw assets stay upstream) and encrypted (off-grid full pack, AES-256-GCM minimum). Compatible runtimes (docs/LIFE_RUNTIME_STANDARD.md) MUST verify each entry in contents[] by sha256 at load, refuse to mount after expires_at, poll withdrawal_endpoint, honour forbidden_uses[], and always identify the result as an AI digital life instance — never as the underlying human.

schema_version.description: Version of the .life file format. Independent of DLRS repo version. v0.1.0 = pointer mode + encrypted mode framework, opaque signature_ref, no cryptographic signature scheme yet (deferred to v0.2).

package_id.description: Unique identifier for this package emission. Crockford base32 ULID (26 chars). A fresh ULID MUST be generated on each build; rebuilding the same source record produces a new package_id even when contents are byte-identical.

mode.description: Distribution mode. pointer (default) keeps raw assets in upstream object storage and ships only *.pointer.json files inside pointers/. encrypted bundles AEAD-sealed blobs inside encrypted/ for off-grid use; the encryption block MUST then be present.

record_id.description: Source DLRS record identifier (humans/<id>/manifest.json::record_id). The .life is a packaged subset of this record.

created_at.description: RFC 3339 / ISO 8601 UTC timestamp at which this package was built.

expires_at.description: RFC 3339 / ISO 8601 UTC timestamp after which compatible runtimes MUST refuse to mount this package. Bounded-time licence: a .life never grants indefinite operating rights.

issued_by.description: Who authorised the emission of this package. role distinguishes self-issued from representative-issued from memorial. signature_ref is opaque in v0.1 (a path or URI); a cryptographic signature scheme is deferred to life-format v0.2.

role.description: self = the subject issued the package themselves. authorized_representative = a legal representative (guardian, agent under POA). memorial_executor = post-mortem only; requires verification_level: memorial_authorized AND a live withdrawal_endpoint.

identifier.description: Opaque human-readable identifier of the issuer (e.g. handle, role title, organisation name).

signature_ref.description: Reference to the issuer's signature artefact. Path inside the .life (e.g. consent/signature.bin) or external URI. Opaque in v0.1; v0.2 will define a concrete signature format.

consent_evidence_ref.description: Path (inside the .life zip) or external URI pointing to the consent document covering this package. Path examples: consent/consent.md, consent/consent.json. URI examples: https://example.org/consent/abc123. Compatible runtimes MAY require this to be inline (path inside .life) for offline operation.

verification_level.description: Strength of identity verification at issuance time. self_attested = subject attested without third-party verification. third_party_verified = an independent verifier (notary, KYC provider) confirmed identity. memorial_authorized = post-mortem package authorised by an executor; requires issued_by.role == memorial_executor.

withdrawal_endpoint.description: URI runtimes MUST poll at session start AND at least every 24h thereafter. On withdrawal, the runtime MUST complete the current turn and terminate the session. Network-unreachable endpoints in offline operation MUST cause the runtime to refuse to mount unless explicitly authorised by the issuer for offline use.

runtime_compatibility.description: Which runtime interfaces this package declares conformance to. Minimum is dlrs-runtime-v0. Additional entries declare extended interfaces (e.g. openai-chat-tool, vrm-1.0, audio-tts-v1). A runtime that cannot satisfy all declared interfaces MUST refuse to mount.

ai_disclosure.description: Minimum disclosure level the runtime MUST apply to every output produced from this package. Mirrors the v0.4 ai_disclosure enum. visible_label_required is the absolute minimum for .life — invisible-only is forbidden.

forbidden_uses.description: Use cases the runtime MUST refuse to generate, regardless of user prompt. The list SHOULD include impersonation_for_fraud, political_endorsement, and explicit_content where applicable; additional use cases MAY be specified per package. Empty array is permitted but strongly discouraged.

audit_event_ref.description: Reference to the package_emitted event inside the bundled audit/events.jsonl. Format: audit/events.jsonl#L<n> where <n> is the 1-based line number. The line's prev_hash MUST chain correctly with prior events; the runtime verifies this at load.

encryption.description: Encryption metadata. Required iff mode == "encrypted"; MUST be absent when mode == "pointer". The package itself never carries unwrapped keys — recipients[].wrapped_key_ref points to the wrapped key blob and the unwrapping key is distributed out-of-band (KMS).

algorithm.description: AEAD algorithm. AES-256-GCM is the v0.1 minimum; ChaCha20-Poly1305 is acceptable for resource-constrained runtimes. No non-AEAD modes permitted.

key_distribution.description: How recipients obtain unwrapped keys. external_kms = recipients fetch unwrapped keys from a KMS using the wrapped_key_ref blob. v0.1 only specifies external_kms; future revisions MAY add direct_recipient_pubkey and similar.

recipients.description: List of authorised key recipients. Each entry binds a key id to the wrapped-key blob inside encrypted/. At least one recipient is required.

kid.description: Key identifier the runtime/KMS uses to look up the unwrapping key.

wrapped_key_ref.description: Path (inside the .life zip, under encrypted/) to the wrapped data-encryption key for this recipient.

assets.description: Map from logical asset → sealed blob inside encrypted/. plaintext_sha256 is the sha256 of the *unencrypted* asset; runtimes verify after decrypt.

logical_path.description: Logical path of the asset (e.g. voice/master.wav). Used by the runtime when resolving asset references from manifest.json or pointer descriptors.

blob_path.description: Path inside the .life zip pointing to the AEAD ciphertext blob.

plaintext_sha256.description: sha256 of the *plaintext* asset. Runtime verifies this AFTER decrypt; it is the integrity check on the original bytes.

nonce.description: Base64-encoded AEAD nonce. MUST be unique per asset within a package.

contents.description: Inventory of every regular file inside the .life zip *except* life-package.json itself (which is the manifest of the manifests). Runtimes MUST verify each entry's sha256 + size at load. Used for whole-package integrity.

path.description: Path inside the zip (forward-slash separated, no leading / and no .. segments).

sha256.description: sha256 of the file as stored in the zip (compressed-size after decompression equals size below; sha256 is computed on the decompressed bytes).

notes.description: Free-form human-readable notes. Not parsed by runtimes.

allOf.description: encrypted mode requires the encryption block.
pointer mode forbids the encryption block.
memorial verification_level requires memorial_executor role and vice versa.
